author: discharge_summary_2021.pdf
title: discharge_summary_2021.pdf
text:
City General Hospital — Internal Medicine
Discharge Summary · Demo only — synthetic data, not medical advice. No real PHI.
Patient: Rahul Sharma
MRN: RH-4471
DOB: 1974-02-03
Admission
Admission date: 2021-08-05. Discharge date: 2021-08-09. Mr. Sharma, a 47-year-old man (at time of
admission), was treated for community-acquired pneumonia and has recovered. Diabetes remained stable
during the stay.
Hospital Course
Empiric antibiotics were started. On day 2 the patient developed an adverse drug reaction (see Allergy
Review, page 2). Antibiotics were changed and the reaction resolved. He was discharged in stable condition
with primary-care follow-up.
Discharge Medications
Metformin 500 mg twice daily (continued). Follow up with primary care.


Allergy Review
Page 2 · Allergy documentation · Demo only — synthetic data, not medical advice. No real PHI.
Documented Drug Allergy
Allergy: Penicillin. During this admission the patient developed a reaction to a penicillin-class antibiotic:
rash and breathing difficulty. Severity: severe. Date recorded: 2021-08-06. Recorded by: Dr. A. Adams.
Clinical instruction: AVOID penicillin-class antibiotics (including amoxicillin, ampicillin) and review
beta-lactam alternatives before prescribing.
